text:
The Olympics 2024 kick off today in Paris, the City of Lights, and we can think of no better way to mark the occasion than with cooking up some French foodie classics.

Whether you're anxiously following Ireland's Olympic track and field team, or simply watching the gymnastics for the outfits, the experience will be even more enjoyable with a homemade tarte tatin, beef bourguignon or a simple crepe accompanying you.

Crème caramel



Classic coq au vin



Nico's grilled pear tarte tatin



Wade Murphy's baked elderflower crème brulée tart



Neven's classic beef bourguignon



Michel Roux's vegetable tart tatin



Chocolate eclairs



Tartiflette



French onion soup



Almond and raspberry frangipani tart



Mark Moriarty's ratatouille tart with halloumi



Crepe Suzette



Classic omelette



French apple tart



Karen Coakley's moules frites



Search by ingredient, chef or programme

title: 15 French-inspired dishes to make to celebrate the Olympics
description: Whether you're anxiously following Ireland's Olympic track and field team, or simply watching the gymnastics for the outfits, mark the start of the tournament with French cuisine.
